0196214137158.name: Pokémon TCG ME03 Perfect Order sealed product
0196214137158.note: EAN validated. External retailers list this EAN for German Perfect Order booster/display; Cardmarket has several matching sealed-product pages, so the system returns candidates rather than guessing silently.

candidates.name: Perfect Order Booster
candidates.note: Most likely if scanning a single booster barcode. Some retailers also use the same EAN for display listings, so verify packaging.

candidates.name: Perfect Order Booster Box
candidates.note: Include when scanning display/box packaging or when retailer mapped the same EAN to a display.

candidates.name: Perfect Order Booster Box (18 Boosters)
candidates.note: Returned as a lower-confidence set candidate because the barcode alone may not distinguish packaging variants.

0196214139299.name: Pokémon TCG Lumiose/Illumina City Mini Tin sealed product
0196214139299.note: EAN validates. External German retailers identify this as Pokémon Illumina City Mini Tin (DE). Cardmarket catalog names the products Lumiose City and splits the five tin artworks into separate products, so all artwork variants are returned. If scanning a sealed display, choose the display candidate instead.

candidates.name: Lumiose City: Salamence Mini Tin
candidates.note: One of five possible Mini Tin artwork variants. Barcode alone may not identify artwork.

candidates.name: Lumiose City: Meganium Mini Tin
candidates.note: One of five possible Mini Tin artwork variants. Barcode alone may not identify artwork.

candidates.name: Lumiose City: Emboar Mini Tin
candidates.note: One of five possible Mini Tin artwork variants. Barcode alone may not identify artwork.

candidates.name: Lumiose City: Feraligatr Mini Tin
candidates.note: One of five possible Mini Tin artwork variants. Barcode alone may not identify artwork.

candidates.name: Lumiose City: Gallade Mini Tin
candidates.note: One of five possible Mini Tin artwork variants. Barcode alone may not identify artwork.

candidates.name: Lumiose City: Mini Tin Display
candidates.note: Use this if you scanned a complete display/case rather than a single mini tin.
